channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Sem querer apoio público para Flávio, o que bolsonaristas esperam de Trump?
item.description: Bolsonarist sources in the U.S. say they do not expect—or even want—Donald Trump to publicly endorse Flávio Bolsonaro’s 2026 presidential bid, despite the family name. Their strategy appears to favor political independence and avoid direct associations that could provoke controversy or create dependence on foreign figures.

item.title: “Turma do Macallan” domina memes e é alvo de protesto no Congresso
item.description: A Macallan whisky tasting event in London in April 2024, sponsored by Banco Master and attended by Brazilian officials including Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes and bank founder Daniel Vorcaro, sparked controversy after cost details were revealed, turning into social media memes. Senator Cleitinho (Republicanos-MG) protested in Congress by bringing a Macallan bottle to the Senate floor and called for the impeachment of STF ministers, alleging misuse of public funds.

item.title: PT prepara ofensiva contra Flávio para usá-la “no momento certo”
item.description: The Workers' Party (PT) is preparing a major offensive against Flávio Bolsonaro, the PL's pre-candidate, to be deployed at the 'right moment,' despite his rising poll numbers tied to the Bolsonaro surname. The strategy aims to link him to corruption (the 'rachadinhas' scandal), militia ties, and labor rights rollbacks, while PT figures like Maria do Rosário and Wellington Dias downplay his surge and assert Lula leads in all scenarios, with a political climate improved since 2022.

item.title: PL vê lado positivo em decisão que revogou visto de assessor de Trump
item.description: Members of the PL party see a positive side in the Brazilian government's decision to revoke the visa of Darren Beattie, a senior advisor at the U.S. State Department, believing it could indirectly benefit Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, Lula's main rival in the 2026 presidential race. They argue the move may heighten tensions with the more ideological wing of the U.S. government and potentially interfere with a planned meeting between Lula and Trump in March.

item.title: Em livro, Lula diz como mudou de opinião sobre CLT e legado de Getúlio
item.description: In the book, President Lula reveals that, during his years as a union leader, he was opposed to the CLT regime, but changed his mind after reflecting on the legacy of Getúlio Vargas, considered the 'father' of the proposal. He acknowledges the importance of the CLT for workers and admits his view evolved over time and through political experience.

item.title: Vorcaro: novo advogado defendeu Braga Netto e negociou delação na Lava Jato
item.description: Lawyer José Luis Oliveira Lima, known as Juca, took over the defense of Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, after previously defending General Braga Netto in the coup plot and former minister José Dirceu in the mensalão trial. He also represented Léo Pinheiro of OAS in Operation Car Wash, whose plea deal implicated Lula and José Yunes. Negotiations for a potential plea bargain by Vorcaro with the PGR are in early stages, while the STF maintains a majority in favor of keeping him imprisoned, awaiting the vote of Gilmar Mendes.

item.title: Flávio começa a rodar o Brasil para oficializar alianças e testar discurso
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) begins his first nationwide campaign as a pre-candidate for the presidency with a visit to Ji-Paraná (RO), following an international tour. Despite the recent hospitalization of his father, former President Jair Bolsonaro, the trip proceeded as planned, marking the start of his effort to build alliances and test his political message across Brazil.

item.title: Leite: ‘Respeito Caiado e Ratinho, mas minha jornada me permite oferecer um caminho independente’
item.description: Eduardo Leite, governor of Rio Grande do Sul and PSD pre-candidate for president, claims he is better positioned than Ratinho Júnior and Ronaldo Caiado to lead an independent candidacy against both Lulismo and Bolsonarism, advocating for a third pole. He emphasizes that he avoided polarization in his RS re-election and refuses to commit to an alliance with Flávio Bolsonaro, despite acknowledging a potential Lula-Bolsonaro runoff. Leite argues his trajectory allows him to offer an alternative path, even as the party appears to favor Ratinho Júnior as its official candidate.

item.title: Moreira Franco, cacique do MDB, critica ‘salada’ de alianças do partido
item.description: Moreira Franco, a leading figure in the MDB, criticized the party's 'salad' of alliances across states, citing Rio de Janeiro where gubernatorial candidate Eduardo Paes (PSD) supports Lula while his running mate Jane Reis (MDB) is tied to bolsonarism. He argued that this ideological ambiguity prevents the party from forming clear policies and described the alliance-building environment as confused and unhealthy.

item.title: Enviado de Trump cria novo ponto de tensão entre EUA e Brasil
item.description: Donald Trump's special adviser for Brazil-related affairs, Darren Beattie, plans to visit imprisoned former president Jair Bolsonaro, creating a new point of tension between the U.S. and Brazil. His agenda includes a meeting with Bolsonaro at the Papudinha prison and participation in a critical minerals event in São Paulo. Beattie is an open critic of the Lula government and has accused Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes of leading a censorship plot against Bolsonaro.

item.title: Iris Marín, defensora del Pueblo de Colombia: “Hay zonas del país donde prima la gobernanza criminal”
item.description: Colombian senator and presidential candidate Miguel Uribe Turbay was assassinated on June 7 during an event in Bogotá after being shot in the head and left knee. He remained in critical condition for two months before dying on August 11, before the formal start of election campaigns, in an incident that signaled rising political violence in the country. The attack underscores the presence of criminal governance in some regions of Colombia.

item.title: El ex primer ministro de Francia Édouard Philippe se juega su futuro político en las elecciones municipales
item.description: Édouard Philippe, former French Prime Minister and incumbent mayor of Le Havre, is running for re-election in the municipal elections this Sunday and March 22. His performance is critical, as a loss could severely damage his presidential ambitions for 2027 against the far-right. Once a strong figure in polls, his support has waned due to off-timing remarks and a less compelling political platform.

item.title: ¿Qué dicen las encuestas? La mayoría del PP en Castilla y León y otras sorpresas
item.description: Polling data for the Castilla y León elections show the PP leading with 33% of the vote, followed by PSOE (29%) and Vox (20%). While the PP is projected to win 31-32 seats, this falls short of the 42-seat absolute majority, requiring a coalition. The most likely scenario is a PP-Vox alliance, which secures 42 seats in 97% of simulations.

item.title: Víctor Font, el tozudo que insiste en unir al barcelonismo
item.description: Víctor Font, a candidate in the 2021 Barcelona elections, lost to Joan Laporta and initially withdrew from public life. Despite the defeat, his inner circle believed he would return, which he did in 2026 as a candidate again — now more mature and without relying on image consultants, trusting only in persistence as his training.

item.title: Iranianos perdem esperança em mudança de regime após ataques devastadores e indicação de Mojtaba
item.description: Iranians are losing hope for regime change after devastating U.S. and Israeli attacks that targeted civilian infrastructure and caused widespread destruction in Tehran. Despite the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and the appointment of his son Mojtaba Khamenei as successor, the regime has shown resilience, and the foreign bombardment has disillusioned many who once viewed external intervention as a path to change.

item.title: Brasil usa 'princípio da reciprocidade' para barrar assessor de Trump; entenda o que é e como foi usado
item.description: Brazil revoked the visa of Darren Beattie, a Trump adviser, applying the principle of reciprocity after alleging he concealed his true intent to meet with Jair Bolsonaro, claiming instead to attend a rare earth minerals event. President Lula stated Beattie will not be allowed to enter Brazil until U.S. Health Minister Alexandre Padilha is permitted to enter the United States.

item.title: Inquérito das fake news completa 7 anos ainda em uso e em meio a crise sem precedentes no STF
item.description: The fake news inquiry at Brazil's Supreme Court (STF) marks seven years amid an unprecedented crisis of public trust, fueled by allegations linking Justices Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli to the Banco Master scandal and its owner, Daniel Vorcaro. Initially supported for countering anti-democratic moves by former President Jair Bolsonaro, the investigation is now widely criticized for its indefinite duration, lack of clear focus, and threat to legal security, prompting lawyers and law professors to demand its immediate termination.

item.title: Lula reforça discurso de soberania ao barrar americano, mas quer manter canal com Trump
item.description: President Lula reinforced his national sovereignty stance by blocking the entry of Darren Beattie, an adviser to the U.S. government, who sought to visit jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro. The move is framed as a reciprocal action after the U.S. revoked the visa of Brazilian Health Minister Alexandre Padilha, reflecting Brazilian concerns about U.S. interference in domestic politics to support Bolsonaro, while Lula still seeks to maintain diplomatic channels with Trump.

item.title: Governo vê riscos ao mercado financeiro com possível designação do PCC e CV como terroristas
item.description: The Lula government warns that a potential designation of PCC and CV as terrorist organizations by the U.S. under Donald Trump could expose Brazilian firms and the national financial system to unilateral sanctions, including extraterritorial application. Financial institutions might face penalties even without direct knowledge of ties to criminal groups, increasing compliance costs and economic risks.

item.title: Haddad volta a criticar alta de juros, mas evitar culpar integrantes do BC
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad criticized the rise in Brazil's real interest rate since mid-2024, arguing it has negatively impacted public well-being due to high household debt. He avoided blaming current Central Bank directors appointed by Lula, stating the issue is structural and he does not want to 'personalize' the debate. Haddad noted Brazil has recorded its lowest four-year inflation accumulation and mentioned that some sectors favor erring on the side of tighter monetary policy to preserve credibility.

item.title: Romeu Zema rasgou o verbo sobre Vorcaro; Bolsonaro é internado em Brasília
item.description: Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) strongly criticized the STF's decision to maintain the preventive detention of banker Daniel Vorcaro, controller of Banco Master. Meanwhile, former councilman Carlos Bolsonaro announced that former President Jair Bolsonaro was hospitalized in Brasília due to a concerning respiratory condition caused by secretion buildup.

item.title: Lula fala para as pessoas tirarem a bunda da cadeira em vez de usar Ozempic
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) urged people to walk more and get off the couch instead of relying on Ozempic for weight loss. He emphasized that the use of Ozempic is delicate and that medical professionals, not individuals, should guide its proper use. The remarks were made during a public event on Friday, the 13th.

item.title: “Eu vou ser candidato”, confirma Haddad
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad confirmed he will leave the Lula government to run in the 2026 elections as a Workers' Party (PT) candidate. The announcement was made in an interview with the '20 Minutos' program on the Opera Mundi portal, where he stated he intends to participate in the elections. Haddad said he will formally announce his candidacy after resigning from office.

item.title: Governo Lula não permitiu a entrada do assessor de Trump no Brasil para visitar Bolsonaro
item.description: President Lula barred Trump advisor Beattie from entering Brazil to visit Bolsonaro, citing omission and falsification of information in the visa application. The Itamaraty confirmed the visa revocation, stating irregularities in the request.

item.title: Haddad lamenta sair do governo sem conseguir implementar projeto de desenvolvimento de País
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad expressed regret over failing to implement a long-term national development project, which stalled due to inter-ministerial disputes over leadership. He also said he had expected a more favorable political environment for Lula’s 2026 re-election bid, but the situation has deteriorated, and confirmed he will run for governor of São Paulo, with an announcement expected after his departure from the Ministry of Finance, possibly during the 17ª Caravana Federativa.

item.title: 'Eu vou participar das eleições', diz Haddad
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad confirmed he will be the PT's candidate in the 2026 elections, though he did not specify the office. He stated he will announce his candidacy after leaving the ministry, likely to run for governor of São Paulo against Tarcísio de Freitas. Haddad said the political landscape has become more complicated due to Flávio Bolsonaro's rise in polls, prompting him to reconsider his initial plan of only supporting Lula's re-election.

item.title: Fux vai analisar ação do PSD contra regras de eleição indireta no Rio de Janeiro
item.description: The PSD has filed a lawsuit at the Supreme Federal Court against a Rio de Janeiro state law governing the indirect election of governor and vice-governor in case of dual vacancy, arguing that the law invades the Union’s exclusive competence to legislate on electoral law and undermines the secret vote by requiring nominal and open voting. The party also challenges a provision allowing candidates to disengage from public office only 24 hours before the election, which could enable public officials to exploit administrative influence and create unequal conditions. The case will be rapporteured by Minister Luiz Fux.

item.title: Flávio pede orações e jejum “da meia-noite às 6h” por Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro urged supporters to fast from midnight to 6 a.m. and pray for the recovery of his father, former President Jair Bolsonaro, after visiting him in the hospital and noting his weak voice and difficulty maintaining balance. Flávio said he was deeply impacted by his father’s condition and asked for prayers aimed at his recovery and return home.

item.title: Brasil do Povo (13/03/26) | Completo
item.description: The 'Brasil do Povo' program, hosted by Katiuzia Rios and broadcast by RedeTV! on March 13, 2026, provided a full coverage of the main national news, focusing on current political and social topics. Although the content does not specifically detail the 2026 elections, the program's format suggests analysis of recent events that may influence the electoral landscape. The broadcast is part of a daily series of full episodes, keeping the public informed about country events.

item.title: RedeTV! News (13/03/26) | Completo
item.description: This is a RedeTV! News broadcast from March 13, 2026, featuring a roundup of national and international news. Topics include BNDES infrastructure investments, HB20 Cup events, President Lula's participation in an AI summit in New Delhi, and Carnival celebrations in São Paulo with Gustavo Mioto. The video is part of a news program with links to related segments.

item.title: Crusoé: Lula deveria se lembrar por que Padilha teve o visto revogado
item.description: President Lula and Itamaraty provided differing justifications for revoking the diplomatic visa of Darren Beattie, a senior Trump administration official for Brazil-related policies. Itamaraty cited omission and falsification of relevant information about the visit's purpose, while Lula offered a different explanation. The case echoes the prior revocation of Rui Costa Padilha's visa, implying Lula should recall that precedent.

item.title: O arrependimento de Flávio Bolsonaro sobre a CPI do caso Master
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro said he regretted supporting the creation of a parliamentary inquiry commission (CPI) into the Banco Master scandal, claiming it would be illegal and politically motivated against him. Analysts suggest his reversal may stem from discovering serious information after signing the request, despite his legal background as a lawyer and senator.

item.title: Após STF manter prisão, Vorcaro troca de advogado
item.description: Daniel Vorcaro, former president of Banco Master, changed lawyers hours after the STF formed a majority to maintain his imprisonment. The new defender, José Luís de Oliveira Lima, previously defended figures such as José Dirceu, Alberto Youssef, and Pedro Guimarães, and is a member of the Innocence Project Brasil. The shift suggests the beginning of negotiations for a possible plea bargain, while Roberto Podval and Sérgio Leonardo remain on the legal team.

item.title: La justicia venezolana niega el derecho a la amnistía a Perkins Rocha, el abogado de María Corina Machado
item.description: Perkins Rocha, legal advisor to Vente Venezuela and close ally of María Corina Machado, had his request for dismissal under Venezuela's recent Amnesty Law denied by a court, despite being under house arrest since his detention in the post-election crackdown of 2024. The ruling is a setback for the Venezuelan opposition, as Rocha is one of Machado's key legal defenders.

item.title: Fábrica da BYD na Bahia acumula encomendas de 100 mil carros para Argentina e México
item.description: BYD's factory in Camaçari (BA) has already received orders for 100,000 vehicles from Argentina and Mexico, with 50,000 units for each country, company executives announced on Friday, March 13. The plant's current capacity is 150,000 vehicles per year, with plans to expand up to 600,000 annually. BYD also announced the construction of a research center in Rio de Janeiro, with a R$300 million investment, expected to be completed by 2028.

item.title: Justiça solta vereador preso por suspeita de negociar campanha com o Comando Vermelho
item.description: Judge Marcus Basilio of the 7th Criminal Chamber of the Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice revoked the arrest of councilman Salvino Oliveira (PSD), who was detained on suspicion of negotiating his electoral campaign with the Red Command faction. The ruling was issued on Friday afternoon, the 13th, following his arrest by the Civil Police on Wednesday, the 11th.

item.title: Rogério Marinho fala aos trainees em jornalismo do Poder360
item.description: Senator Rogério Marinho (PL-RN) delivered a lecture to the second cohort of Poder360's Journalism Trainee Program in Brasília, discussing the role of the Opposition and key disputes in the 2026 general elections. Marinho, considered the likely campaign coordinator for pre-candidate Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), emphasized topics such as the economy and institutional politics before aspiring journalists.

item.title: Visita ao Poder360: Rogério Marinho
item.description: Senator Rogério Marinho (PL-RN) visited the Poder360 headquarters in Brasília to deliver a lecture to journalism trainees, discussing the 2026 elections and the role of the opposition in Brazil's political landscape.

item.title: Moraes determina prisão definitiva de 7 integrantes do 'núcleo 3', composto pelos 'kids pretos'
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has ordered the definitive imprisonment of seven members of the so-called 'núcleo 3', a group involved in the coup plot to keep Jair Bolsonaro in power after his 2022 election defeat. Known as the 'black kids', they were linked to efforts to block the inauguration of Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.

item.title: Papo Antagonista: Lula proíbe assessor de Trump de entrar no Brasil
item.description: The Lula administration barred entry to Brazil for a Donald Trump advisor, reportedly in connection with an attempted visit to the hospitalized former president Jair Bolsonaro. The report also notes that Alexandre de Moraes ordered searches, without specifying the target. Papo Antagonista additionally discusses the frontrunners for the 2026 Oscars.

item.title: Kassab: “O candidato ainda não foi definido”
item.description: The national president of the PSD, Gilberto Kassab, stated that the party has not yet defined its presidential candidate for the 2026 elections. While he mentioned the names of governors Ronaldo Caiado (GO), Ratinho Júnior (PR), and Eduardo Leite (RS) as potential pre-candidates, Kassab said the decision will be made only at the end of the month. The party is keeping the selection process open, prioritizing internal dialogue before an official announcement.

item.title: O pontapé inicial na pré-candidatura de Haddad em São Paulo
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad is expected to announce his pre-candidacy for governor of São Paulo next Thursday at the 17th Federative Caravan, with President Lula and Minister Simone Tebet present. He faces a difficult challenge, as a Datafolha poll shows Governor Tarcísio de Freitas leading him by 15 points in a potential runoff. Haddad’s running mate has not yet been determined.

item.title: Bolsonaro está estável, mas situação é extremamente grave, diz médico
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro is hospitalized in the ICU at DF Star hospital with bacterial broncopneumonia in both lungs, caused by gastric fluid aspiration due to reflux. Although he shows stability, doctors described his condition as 'extremely grave', posing a risk to his life. Michelle Bolsonaro and his sons Flávio, Carlos, Jair Renan, and Laura were authorized to visit him by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes.

item.title: Moraes determina prisão definitiva de sete integrantes do ‘núcleo 3’, composto pelos ‘kids pretos’
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes ordered the definitive imprisonment of seven members of the 'núcleo 3', a coup plotter group composed of military personnel known as 'kids pretos' and a Federal Police agent, convicted of plotting the assassination of authorities, including Moraes himself, Vice President Alckmin, and President Lula. Sentences range from 16 to 24 years in prison, with the individuals transferred to Army detention facilities after all appeals were exhausted. The ruling confirms the severity of the plot, which included messages about 'killing half the world' to keep Bolsonaro in power.

item.title: Flávio pede jejum por Bolsonaro da meia-noite às 6h da manhã
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro urged supporters to fast from midnight to 6 a.m. and pray for his father Jair Bolsonaro’s recovery, who is hospitalized in the ICU with bilateral bacterial pneumonia of probable aspiration origin. He described Jair Bolsonaro as weak, with a soft voice and unable to perform physiotherapy due to medication side effects, and reiterated his call for house arrest to allow continuous family or medical care.

item.title: Justiça solta vereador Salvino Barbosa por prova “precária”
item.description: Councilman Salvino Barbosa (PSD-RJ) was released after the Rio de Janeiro State Court ruled the evidence against him in operation Contenção Red Legacy was 'precarious,' citing no direct contact with the criminal faction CV. He had been arrested on suspicion of negotiating kiosk authorizations in Gardênia Azul with a CV leader in exchange for political support, but denied any links and claimed to be a victim of injustice. He remains under precautionary measures, including a ban on contact with other suspects and passport retention.

item.title: Brasil, México e Colômbia pedem cessar-fogo e retomada de negociações no Oriente Médio
item.description: Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia issued a joint statement calling for an immediate ceasefire and resumption of diplomatic negotiations in the Middle East following U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran on February 28, which have resulted in over 2,000 deaths. The three countries advocate for resolving state disagreements through diplomacy and international mechanisms, offering to support peace-building efforts. The statement follows phone conversations between Lula and the presidents of Colombia and Mexico, though official records did not specify discussion of the conflict.

item.title: Apoio de Trump a Flávio Bolsonaro beneficiaria mais Lula do que o próprio senador, diz pesquisa
item.description: A poll suggests that if Donald Trump endorses Flávio Bolsonaro's presidential bid, it would more likely boost Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's re-election chances than benefit Bolsonaro himself. Trump's endorsement could energize moderate and anti-bolsonarista voters, indirectly strengthening Lula's campaign.

item.title: Brasil revoga visto de assessor de Trump que se encontraria com Bolsonaro
item.description: Brazil revoked the visa of Darren Beattie, a senior adviser at the U.S. Department of State, after discovering he misrepresented the purpose of his trip, claiming he would attend a conference on critical minerals while actually planning to visit imprisoned former President Jair Bolsonaro and meet with Flávio Bolsonaro, a 2026 presidential pre-candidate. The Itamaraty deemed the omission sufficient grounds for visa denial, citing undue interference in Brazil’s internal affairs during an election year.

item.title: Entenda a broncopneumonia, doença que levou Bolsonaro à UTI
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was admitted to the ICU with bacterial bronchopneumonia, exhibiting high fever, dropping oxygen saturation, and respiratory distress. His son, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, attributed the hospitalization to aspiration of gastric fluids during hiccups, linking the severity to prison conditions and requesting house arrest, though medical experts stress that such a causal link cannot be confirmed with certainty.
